body.objective: Build the Swarm Intelligence Kernel that links goals, loop-runs, panels, claims, capabilities and worker leases while separating registry state from active execution.
constraints: Active execution requires runtime evidence.
Do not count registry agents, taskCount or prepared leases as running workers.
No automatic worker start during model creation.
acceptance_criteria: State model supports observed, hypothesized, planned, queued, prepared, running, checking, ready_for_human_merge, completed, blocked, rejected and escalated.
Illegal transitions are rejected.
Swarm status distinguishes registry_agent_count, live_agent_count, prepared_leases, running_leases and active_execution_count.

body.objective: Build a typed capability registry for skills, specialist agents, runtime adapters, deterministic harnesses, memory sources and dashboard actions.
constraints: Draft and candidate capabilities are advisory only.
Validated capabilities may route live workers only within risk ceiling.
Every capability needs a removal strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Capability contracts include allowed actions, forbidden actions, required evidence, risk ceiling, eval threshold and removal strategy.
Routing refuses draft, disabled, over-risk or below-threshold capabilities.
Tests cover skill contract failure and live-routing refusal.

body.objective: Build specialist councils and a hypothesis workbench for bounded multi-disciplinary reasoning, dissent capture and backlog projection.
constraints: Specialist profiles are bounded contracts, not ungoverned personas.
High-risk panels require security_reviewer.
Panel-to-backlog projection must not start workers.
acceptance_criteria: Catalog includes mathematician, physicist, biologist, psychologist, behavioral_scientist, philosopher, security_reviewer, systems_architect, product_strategist and data_scientist.
Panel consensus preserves support, oppose, uncertain, needs_evidence and dissent.
Hypotheses include evidence plan, falsification signal and stop condition.

body.objective: Build an evidence graph and claim ledger linking goals, loop-runs, worker leases, panels, capabilities, sources, claims, decisions, memory candidates and trace spans.
constraints: Unsupported claims remain proposed.
Contradictions must be visible.
Policy memory requires human review.
Secret-like evidence is rejected before persistence.
acceptance_criteria: Claims support proposed, supported, contradicted, resolved, rejected and promoted states.
Evidence graph supports lineage from backlog to panel to goal to lease to memory candidate.
Tests cover contradiction links, secret-like rejection and policy memory review gate.

body.objective: Build Capacity Governor v2 with queue classes, fair-share scheduling, runtime concurrency, token budget, wall-clock budget, failure budget and kill handling.
constraints: Planning must explain eligible and blocked work before starts.
Fair-share scheduling must prevent one queue class from starving others.
Kill handling must preserve stdout, stderr, trace and checkpoint evidence.
High-risk work remains security/human-gated.
acceptance_criteria: Runner plan groups work by queue class, runtime, risk and age.
Blocked leases include policy, runtime, capacity, token, wall-clock, failure-budget or gate reason.
Tests cover fair-share ordering, exhausted budgets, repeated failures, runtime unavailable and killed worker evidence.

body.objective: Build evaluation harnesses for skill contracts, specialist outputs, memory retrieval, routing decisions, worker outcomes and dashboard truth.
constraints: Deterministic gates override advisory LLM evals.
Eval scorecards must include evidence refs.
Known-bad fixtures must fail.
acceptance_criteria: Skill contract eval blocks invalid or over-risk capabilities.
Specialist output eval counts unsupported claims.
Dashboard truth eval fails if prepared leases are labeled as active execution.
Routing eval records why each capability was selected or blocked.

body.objective: Build Mission Control dashboard views for swarm truth, capacity, specialist councils, skill catalog, claim ledger, evidence graph and next safe action.
constraints: Dashboard must label MacBook cockpit versus workstation execution node.
Actions for non-validated capabilities must be disabled.
Decision lineage must link backlog, panel, reviews, evidence, goal and leases.
acceptance_criteria: Mission Control shows registry, active execution, queue depth, capacity, budgets and blocked reasons.
Specialist Council view shows panel state, consensus, dissent and evidence refs.
Skill Catalog view shows status, eval score, risk ceiling, allowed actions and blocked reason.
Claim Ledger view filters contradicted, review-required and promoted claims.

body.objective: Build the decision governance harness for evaluator quorum, split-decision eligibility, audit manifests, replay lineage, runtime warning gates, circuit breakers and merge-ready closure semantics.
constraints: Quorum is a hard gate for high-risk or disputed work.
Split-decision must be policy-eligible and must not create leases.
Runner start, skip, fail, stop and kill decisions require audit manifests.
Replay branches must not copy historical worker leases.
ready_for_human_merge is not completed for mutating work.
acceptance_criteria: High-risk work with missing quorum remains blocked.
Split request is denied unless too-large, repeated-failure or checker-recommended evidence exists.
Runner decisions include decision id, policy version, runtime contract, capacity snapshot, budget snapshot and gate refs.
Replay run references source checkpoint and starts with zero leases.
Circuit breaker blocks repeated OpenCode timeouts or maker/checker failures with evidence preserved.

body.objective: Run an end-to-end bounded swarm scenario from question to specialist panel to backlog to goal to prepared leases to policy-gated worker run to evidence graph and memory candidate.
constraints: Start with mock runtime before Codex/OpenCode.
No merge, push or deploy.
No automatic durable memory promotion.
Record endpoint, dashboard, trace, artifact, budget and remaining-risk evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario proves question intake, hypothesis, specialist panel, consensus, backlog projection, goal creation and prepared leases.
Policy-gated low-risk execution completes with maker/checker evidence.
Evidence graph links every step.
Dashboard shows active execution only when runtime evidence exists.
Smoke evidence records runtime status, token usage, wall-clock usage, artifacts and blocked high-risk paths.
